Title: Sticking to cellulose: exploiting Arabidopsis seed coat mucilage to understand cellulose biosynthesis and cell wall polysaccharide interactions

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal mucilage adherent layer
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structuring network for mucilage adherence and cell wall cohesion
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose crystallinity and adherent mucilage; mutation reduces cellulose crystallinity, reduces cellulose in adherent mucilage, and causes loss of mucilage adherence
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; cellulose staining; crystalline cellulose measurement
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed during seed coat epidermal differentiation/seed development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COBL2 mutation causes an almost identical mucilage phenotype to cesa5. 2. COBL2 is required for normal cellulose crystallinity in seed mucilage. 3. COBL2 supports adherence of pectin-rich mucilage through proper cellulose organization.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes published evidence; COBL2 is presented as a confirmed actor in mucilage cellulose biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing seed coat
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modify cellulose organization and mucilage adherence in seed coat cell walls
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed developmental expression data from Winter et al. 2007 (no accession reported)

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal mucilage adherent layer/rays
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cell wall cohesion and mucilage architecture
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for mucilage adherence, cellulose ray structures, and proper epidermal cell morphology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STELLO1 affects mucilage adherence and cellulose ray structures. 2. Its role is consistent with a conserved function in CSC assembly/trafficking. 3. Seed coat phenotypes support involvement in cellulose biosynthesis-related processes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported together with STELLO2; known from hypocotyls to be required for CSC assembly and trafficking
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to alter cellulose complex assembly in specialized cell walls
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed developmental expression data from Winter et al. 2007 (no accession reported)

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal mucilage adherent layer/rays
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cell wall cohesion and mucilage architecture
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for mucilage adherence, cellulose ray structures, and proper epidermal cell morphology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STELLO2 affects mucilage adherence and cellulose ray structures. 2. Its role is consistent with a conserved function in CSC assembly/trafficking. 3. Seed coat phenotypes support involvement in cellulose biosynthesis-related processes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported together with STELLO1; known from hypocotyls to be required for CSC assembly and trafficking
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to alter cellulose complex assembly in specialized cell walls
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed developmental expression data from Winter et al. 2007 (no accession reported)

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal mucilage adherent layer
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains cellulose organization and pectin adherence in mucilage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for cellulose ray formation and mucilage adherence; mutation causes repartitioning of pectin to nonadherent mucilage and loss of cellulosic rays while diffuse cellulose remains
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; double-mutant analysis with cesa5; cellulose staining; mucilage partition analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOS5 defines a cellulose-related mucilage phenotype distinct from cesa5. 2. sos5 mutants lose cellulosic rays but retain diffuse cellulose. 3. sos5 enhances cesa5, indicating an independent pathway controlling mucilage adherence.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanism remains unresolved; may act structurally in the apoplast rather than through signaling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for altering wall polymer organization and seed mucilage properties
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt-independent in seed mucilage phenotype; seed hydration/imbibition
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Linked in other tissues to salt/sucrose-sensitive growth defects

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal mucilage adherent layer
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains cellulose organization and pectin adherence in mucilage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper cellulose ray distribution and mucilage adherence; fei2 phenotype resembles sos5 with loss of rays and pectin repartitioning
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; genetic interaction analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FEI2 functions in the same pathway as SOS5 for mucilage adherence. 2. fei2 mutants show absence of cellulosic rays while diffuse cellulose remains. 3. FEI2 acts independently from CESA5-mediated cellulose production pathway.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Competing hypotheses propose signaling via ACS5 or a structural wall-organizing role
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying extracellular wall architecture
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Linked in roots to salt/sucrose-sensitive growth phenotypes

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal mucilage adherent layer
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports proper mucilage architecture through galactoglucomannan production
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation causes compact adherent mucilage and reduced cellulose staining/crystalline cellulose while rays and diffuse cellulose remain
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; cellulose staining; birefringence/crystalline cellulose analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUCI10 contributes to the GGM-dependent class of mucilage cellulose phenotypes. 2. Mutation reduces crystalline cellulose and compacts the adherent mucilage layer. 3. MUCI10 likely affects cellulose organization indirectly through GGM.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role discussed in context of GGM-mediated effects on cellulose crystallinity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning hemicellulose-cellulose interactions in cell walls

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase catalytic subunit, glycosyltransferase family 2
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary wall-like cellulose synthesis in seed coat mucilage/adherent layer
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that assemble into cellulose microfibrils
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in mucilage cellulose production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed during seed coat epidermal differentiation and mucilage biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; GFP localization/live imaging; cellulose staining; crystalline cellulose measurement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal adherent mucilage layer and rays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides scaffold for adherent mucilage organization and pectin attachment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes mucilage adherence and crystalline cellulose accumulation; mutation causes loss of diffuse cellulose staining, retention of rays, and pectin repartitioning from adherent to nonadherent mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA5 is a principal cellulose synthase required for mucilage adherence. 2. cesa5 mutation reduces seed crystalline cellulose and disrupts adherent mucilage organization. 3. CESA5 moves unidirectionally in seed coat epidermal cells during mucilage biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Seed developmental expression data from Winter et al. 2007 (no accession reported)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing seed coat
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target to engineer cellulose scaffold properties and pectin adherence in seed coat cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity is regulated by phosphorylation in hypocotyls in response to light; likely part of mucilage CSC with CESA3 and possibly CESA1/CESA10

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase catalytic subunit, glycosyltransferase family 2
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary wall-like cellulose synthesis in seed coat mucilage/adherent layer
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains for cellulose microfibril formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in mucilage cellulose production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Missense mutant phenotype analysis (ixr1-1, ixr1-2); GFP localization/live imaging; cellulose staining; crystallinity analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal adherent mucilage layer and rays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cellulose scaffold for mucilage structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper ray structures, pectin partitioning, and cellulose crystallinity in mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA3 missense alleles produce mucilage phenotypes. 2. CESA3 affects ray architecture, pectin repartitioning, and cellulose crystallinity. 3. CESA3 is a major component of the mucilage cellulose synthase machinery.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune cellulose structure/crystallinity in specialized walls
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Isoxaben resistance-associated substitutions may alter broader cellulose-related growth traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: cesa3 ixr1 alleles alter cellulose crystallinity; participates in reduced-velocity unidirectional CSC movement in SCE

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds xylosyl side-chains to RG-I in seed mucilage
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for pectin-cellulose linkage in mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; mucilage composition/function analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal adherent mucilage layer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Links RG-I pectin to cellulose for mucilage adherence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes mucilage adherence by enabling xylosyl side-chains that bind RG-I to cellulose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUM5 is required for addition of xylosyl side-chains to RG-I. 2. These side-chains directly contribute to binding RG-I to cellulose. 3. mum5 mutants phenocopy the cesa5-type adhesion defect.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required for synthesis of xylan associated with mucilage RG-I and cellulose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for adherent mucilage integrity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; cellulose staining; birefringence/crystalline cellulose analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal adherent mucilage layer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports linkage of pectin to cellulose and cellulose crystallization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for xylan synthesis, adherent mucilage retention, and normal cellulose staining/crystalline cellulose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX7 is required for xylan synthesis in mucilage. 2. irx7 mutants show nearly complete loss of adherent mucilage. 3. IRX7-dependent xylan contributes both to pectin-cellulose linkage and crystalline cellulose formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype more severe than cesa5 or mum5; both rays and diffuse cellulose are lost on agitation

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase involved in xylan backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required for synthesis of xylan associated with mucilage RG-I and cellulose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for adherent mucilage integrity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; cellulose staining; birefringence/crystalline cellulose analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal adherent mucilage layer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports linkage of pectin to cellulose and cellulose crystallization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for xylan synthesis, adherent mucilage retention, and normal cellulose staining/crystalline cellulose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRX14 is required for xylan synthesis in mucilage. 2. irx14 mutants exhibit almost complete loss of adherent mucilage. 3. Xylan made by IRX14 contributes to both pectin anchoring and crystalline cellulose properties.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotype more severe than cesa5 or mum5; both rays and diffuse cellulose are lost on agitation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the β-1,4-linked mannan/glucan backbone of galactoglucomannan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for GGM-dependent cellulose organization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; cellulose staining; birefringence/crystalline cellulose analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cellulose crystallinity in seed coat mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat epidermal adherent mucilage layer
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports proper cellulose crystallinity and mucilage polymer spacing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation compacts the adherent mucilage layer and reduces cellulose staining and crystalline cellulose, while retaining rays and diffuse cellulose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLA2 is required for GGM production in mucilage. 2. csla2 mutants define a third class of cellulose-related mucilage phenotype. 3. GGM produced by CSLA2 broadly affects crystalline cellulose properties in mucilage.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review suggests effects may reflect altered cellulose crystallinity rather than only polymer spacing
